Title: High-level production of health-beneficial glucoraphanin by multiplex editing of AOP2 gene family in mustard

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Side-chain modification branch converting methylsulfinylalkyl to alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glucoraphanin is health-beneficial; its hydrolysis product sulforaphane has anti-cancer and chemopreventive activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates toward alkenyl glucosinolates; AOP2 activity diverts glucoraphanin precursor pool away from glucoraphanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates such as 4-methylsulfinylbutyl glucosinolate and related homologs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Alkenyl glucosinolates such as gluconapin/sinigrin-related products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for structural outcome of aliphatic glucosinolate profile in this study; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low transcript abundance in most tested tissues; truncated allele due to 213 bp insertion in exon 2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low transcript abundance in most tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Containment net-house/field-grown conditions; analyzed in sprouts, microgreens, young leaves, and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex editing; homologue-specific genotyping by Sanger sequencing/ICE; HPLC glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutations in BjuAOP2.A02 did not further enhance glucoraphanin because this Varuna allele is already truncated/nonfunctional
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident penalty reported at whole-line level
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNA expression; CsVMV promoter for SpCas9; CaMV35S promoter for bar
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Minor direct value as target because this homolog is naturally truncated in Varuna; useful for understanding homologue subfunctionalization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a unique 213 bp insertion in exon 2 causing premature termination in cv. Varuna; editing this homolog showed no additional effect on glucoraphanin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BjuAOP2.A02 is one of five AOP2 homologues identified in Brassica juncea cv. Varuna. 2. It carries a unique 213 bp insertion causing premature termination and likely loss of function. 3. CRISPR-induced mutations in this homolog did not further increase glucoraphanin, indicating negligible contribution in Varuna.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Side-chain modification branch converting methylsulfinylalkyl to alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glucoraphanin is health-beneficial; sulforaphane released from it has anti-cancer and chemopreventive activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to conversion of methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates into alkenyl glucosinolates, reducing glucoraphanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of a homologous enzyme set controlling metabolic branch outcome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Generally low transcript abundance; relatively higher in stem tissue among A-subgenome homologues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low in most tissues, relatively higher in stem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Containment net-house/field-grown conditions; multiple tissue stages tested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex editing; mutation genotyping; HPLC glucosinolate profiling across segregating lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function contributes to glucoraphanin enhancement but alone/with limited edited homologues gives only modest increase
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident penalty reported at whole-line level
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNA expression; CsVMV promoter for SpCas9; CaMV35S promoter for bar
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as one component in multiplex editing stacks for high-glucoraphanin mustard
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Required in combination with additional homolog knockouts for high glucoraphanin; indicative of homologue dose dependence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BjuAOP2.A03 is an expressed AOP2 homologue with relatively low transcript levels except somewhat higher in stem. 2. Frameshift mutation contributes to increasing glucoraphanin. 3. Its knockout alone or with few other homologues is insufficient for maximal glucoraphanin accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Side-chain modification branch converting methylsulfinylalkyl to alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glucoraphanin is health-beneficial; sulforaphane released from it has anti-cancer and chemopreventive activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates toward alkenyl glucosinolates and thus limits glucoraphanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major control homolog within the AOP2 family in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low-to-moderate transcript abundance relative to B-subgenome homologues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Containment net-house/field-grown conditions; multiple tissue types and generations tested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex editing; segregation analysis in transgene-free lines; HPLC glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function had a prominent effect and further enhanced glucoraphanin up to around 60% of total seed glucosinolates when added to edits in other homologues
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident penalty reported at whole-line level
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNA expression; CsVMV promoter for SpCas9; CaMV35S promoter for bar
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority editing target for metabolic engineering of glucoraphanin-rich mustard
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite lower expression than B-subgenome homologues, this homolog showed strong functional importance for glucoraphanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BjuAOP2.A09 is a functionally important AOP2 homologue in Brassica juncea. 2. Its knockout markedly increases glucoraphanin beyond the effect of editing A03/B01/B03 alone. 3. It is one of the homologues with the most prominent influence on the high-glucoraphanin phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Side-chain modification branch converting methylsulfinylalkyl to alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glucoraphanin is health-beneficial; sulforaphane released from it has anti-cancer and chemopreventive activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates toward alkenyl glucosinolates, lowering glucoraphanin and promoting anti-nutritional alkenyl glucosinolate formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major control homolog within family; decisive contributor in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High transcript abundance; one of the more strongly expressed homologues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Containment net-house/field-grown conditions; multiple tissues and generations tested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex editing; mutation profiling; transgene-free segregation analysis; HPLC glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function had one of the most prominent effects on glucoraphanin accumulation and reduction of undesirable alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No compromise in seed quality, yield, or agronomic performance at whole-line level
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNA expression; CsVMV promoter for SpCas9; CaMV35S promoter for bar
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority target for CRISPR editing to create high-glucoraphanin, low-progoitrin mustard
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly expressed B-subgenome homolog; contributes strongly to glucosinolate structural conversion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BjuAOP2.B01 is one of the highly expressed AOP2 homologues in mustard. 2. Its knockout strongly elevates glucoraphanin and reduces anti-nutritional alkenyl glucosinolates. 3. Together with A09, it shows the most prominent contribution to the desired metabolic phenotype.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Side-chain modification branch converting methylsulfinylalkyl to alkenyl glucosinolates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glucoraphanin is health-beneficial; sulforaphane released from it has anti-cancer and chemopreventive activities
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to conversion of methylsulfinylalkyl glucosinolates to alkenyl glucosinolates, thereby limiting glucoraphanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important family member but not alone sufficient for maximal control
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expressed gene across all tested tissue types
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed across all tested tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Containment net-house/field-grown conditions; multiple tissues and generations tested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 multiplex editing; expression analysis; mutation profiling; HPLC glucosinolate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout contributes to glucoraphanin increase and reduction of alkenyl glucosinolates, but maximal phenotype requires additional homolog knockouts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident penalty reported at whole-line level
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtU6-26 promoter for sgRNA expression; CsVMV promoter for SpCas9; CaMV35S promoter for bar
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful multiplex target in editing combinations for elite high-glucoraphanin lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although highest expressed, discussion highlights particularly prominent effects of A09 and B01 knockouts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BjuAOP2.B03 is the most highly expressed AOP2 homologue across tested tissues. 2. Its knockout contributes to increased glucoraphanin and decreased alkenyl glucosinolates. 3. However, maximal high-glucoraphanin phenotype requires simultaneous loss of additional key homologues.
